decks.title: Bolting Retrofit (client deck)
decks.text:
Bolting Foundation: The Process. Provide plan for foundation work as needed. Pull city permit for foundation seismic retrofit. Install Simpson plates UFP-10, bolt into the foundation, 3/8 lag screw into the seal plate per plans (4ft within home perimeter). Replace or adjust all concrete piers and 4x4 posts as needed. Install Simpson metal straps T-128/T-66 for all existing foundation. Install Simpson framing anchor for all concrete piers. Install Simpson framing anchor A-35 every 33".
Cripple Wall Process: Rebuild cripple wall with 2x4 blocking top/bottom and 2x4 studs every 16". Install 1/2" structural plywood per code. Install metal straps every 24" between plywood and wood blocking.
Piers System: Install new 12x12 concrete piers with 4x4 post. Install new 4x6 beams under floor joists every 5 ft to support the floor. Clean up all debris. Call city for final inspection.

decks.title: Pitched Roof (client deck)
decks.text:
3 Types of Pitched Roof work: Overlay (up to two layers of shingles), Tear-off (replacement of the roof without decking), Re-deck (replacement of the deck with plywood). Brands: GAF, Owens Corning, CertainTeed, Malarkey, Timberline.
Signs a roof needs replacement: uneven or buckled shingles, excessive or missing shingles, stains, wood rot or termite damage, attic damage; check the slope and deck type (spaced/filled/solid).
Tear-off vs go-over layer: overlay roofing is like a band-aid, it doesn't fix the problem.
Recipe for a great roof: quality shingles and underlayment, quality labor, protect vulnerable parts (chimney, vents, valleys), good ventilation, warranties, energy-efficient products; licensed and insured, bonded with the best roofing brand.
Tear-Off Process: obtain permits from the city; remove existing roof and haul away debris; replace studs/fascia board/eaves/wood slats as needed; install synthetic underlayment Titanium UDL 50; install metal flashing around fascia board and vent pipes as needed; install Owens Corning Duration asphalt shingles with SureNail Technology; clean up and call for final inspection.
Underlayment options: asphalt-saturated felt, rubberized asphalt, synthetic (recommended: Titanium synthetic).
Recommended shingle: Owens Corning Duration - SureNail Technology, best protection warranty, wind resistance, durability, performance.
Roof Re-Deck: installation of 1/2 inch CDX plywood or OSB over the roof. Ventilation must abide by city code; options: dormer vents, ventilator turbine, ridge vent, gable vent.

decks.title: Mold (client deck)
decks.text:
Why get a mold inspection: visible mold, recent water damage, peace of mind, feeling sick, strange smell, real estate transaction.
Mold test inspection includes: visual inspection, moisture detection equipment, two wall test samples and one air sample, lab results and detailed report, proposal based on report on how to fix if necessary.
Signs and symptoms of mold exposure: sneezing, runny or stuffy nose, cough and postnasal drip, itchy eyes/nose/throat, watery eyes, dry scaly skin.
Remediation scope of work (if mold test positive): set up critical and caution flagging to work area using 6-mil plastic; set up decontamination chamber with negative air filtration to ensure proper air exchange during demolition; remove and properly dispose of all nonstructural mold; remove all mold from exposed framing using abrasive methods (sanding, grinding); wet-wipe and HEPA-vacuum all surfaces within the contained area; apply surface prep cleaning solution; remove the contained room from negative pressure and run HEPA machine on scrub for minimum 48 hours; apply anti-microbial solution to all exposed framing; wipe down all 6-mil plastic and prepare for post-remediation testing; clean the contained area and haul away debris to a professional waste company.
Mold remediation average cost for 50 sf: $9K to $15K.

decks.title: Exterior Paint / Cool Life (client deck)
decks.text:
Benefits of exterior paint: boosts home value by ~5 percent, energy-efficient product can save money, protects the home from harsh weather, maintains structural integrity (mold and mildew can break down the structure), sell your home faster, keeps termites and ants away.
Exterior paint step by step: water-blast the house to remove dust and dirt; mask all windows, shrubbery and driveway as needed; trench 5-6 inches around exterior walls; prepare all walls and patch as needed; sand and scrape all loose paint; prepare all trim, windows, fascia board, eaves and wood doors; patch wood and caulk around windows; replace damaged wood as needed; prime with a coat of primer; apply either regular paint or Cool Life wall coating; clean up and haul away debris. Preparation is the key.

decks.title: Electrical Rewiring (client deck)
decks.text:
Signs a house needs rewiring: flickering lights, aluminum wiring or knob-and-tube (popular in the 60s), electric shocks, outlet problems, persistent circuit-breaker tripping, light bulbs burning out, sparking outlets, regularly blown fuses, discolored outlets or switches, loose wire, shock when touching a cord, overloaded appliances or fixtures.
Rewiring process: obtain a permit; remove existing outlets, switches, fixtures and covers; install new wire - Romex or flex metal pipe (commercial); replace existing wire up to city code (copper, outlets #12, switches #14) and dedicated lines to the main panel as needed; replace outlets and switches (standard model); install new covers; install fixtures, lights or ceiling fans (supplied by customer); clean up and haul away debris; call for inspection. Patching or paint not included unless specified; electric box is not part of rewire unless specified.
Full house rewire scope: obtain permit (homeowner responsible for permit fees); cover all working areas; demolish and remove all existing old wires; open walls as needed to upgrade and relocate electrical systems up to city code; install smoke detectors in every bedroom and hallway on hot wire per code; rewire complete house including kitchen, bathrooms, bedrooms, hallways, dining room, laundry, living room; supply and install new 200-AMP electrical box; supply and install new sub-panel; supply and install AFCI special breakers; final inspection; clean up. Patch and paint optional/not included. Typical price: $25K to $38K.
Main panel upgrade to 200 AMP: obtain permit; call for spot location from utility company; cover work area; demolish and remove old panel; supply and install new 200-AMP panel with AFCI breakers; rough inspection; troubleshoot entire house for shorts; final inspection; repair exterior stucco and paint as needed.

decks.title: Deck (client deck)
decks.text:
Deck material options: pressure-treated lumber, Douglas fir wood, composite decking, Trex composite, synthetic lumber.
Deck scope of work: demo and haul away existing deck surface; prepare ground material as needed for new installation (grade, slope, compaction); supply and install new framing to support the deck surface (size and measurements approved per customer request and pre-job meeting); supply and install new deck surface material (determined at material-selection stage); if wood deck chosen, sand entire surface and paint, stain or seal per customer request; install new railing around deck; clean up and haul away all related debris.
Deck cost: labor only; material runs $25-$60 per unit.

decks.title: Bathroom Remodeling (client deck)
decks.text:
Bathroom remodeling process: company provides permit for the job (permit fee not included); company provides floor plan as needed; demo existing bathroom and haul away debris; replace studs or subfloor as needed; upgrade all electrical up to city code; upgrade all plumbing up to city code; call for rough inspection; shower hot-mop or tub inspection; lathing inspection; float cement on shower walls; install tile; install fixtures and accessories.
The rundown: electric, plumbing, hot mop, drywall, plaster, paint. Hot mop vs waterproofing - both paths available. After inspection, tile is installed around tub or shower; company helps choose design and fixtures; installs shower door, shampoo niche, grout colors per selection.
Vanities: custom vanity or ready-made (30/36/48/60/72 inch).
Cost benchmarks (Remodeling cost-vs-value 2022): midrange bath remodel about $27K-$31K with ~59-67% resale value recoup. Typical range quoted: $25K to $40K.
